meaning: n.(意大利式) 室外地滚球戏
examples: Among them, boccia, goalball, powerlifting and wheelchair rugby are the four unique Paralympic sports.
During the Paralympic Games, the 4800-square-meter banquet hall on the first floor of the main part was used as the venue for wheelchair fencing and boccia events.
Barry College is setting up a boccia club for its students, while the Caerau Bowmen club, based in Barry, says it wants more disabled archers.
James Roe, part of the mixed coxed team who won gold at the Paralympics, has been appointed MBE along with Paralympic boccia player Nigel Murray.
World number one Nigel Murray got off to a winning start as he began the defence of his individual boccia title, beating his first two opponents.
